question.text: The Forward Markets Commission (FMC) was merged with which regulatory body?

answer.text: SEBI — as part of the consolidation of commodity derivative regulation in 2015

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FMC was merged with SEBI when FCRA was repealed and commodity regulation transferred to SEBI under SCRA 1956. **The Trap:** Not merged with RBI, Ministry of Finance, or IRDAI. The specific merger was FMC into SEBI. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers regulatory consolidation in India. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The FMC-SEBI merger brought commodity markets under the same regulatory rigor as equity markets — enhanced investor protection, better surveillance, and unified compliance standards.
